papers.relevance: Daily-use and personal-care products linked to urinary endocrine-disrupting chemical biomarkers.
papers.caution: Observational pilot; exposure biomarkers are not hormonal effects.

papers.relevance: Personal-care product use and urinary phthalate/paraben biomarkers during pregnancy.
papers.caution: Self-reported use in a fertility-clinic population; association is not causation.

papers.relevance: Adult-child personal-care product use and paired urinary phenol/phthalate measurements.
papers.caution: Small cohort and many product-chemical comparisons.

papers.relevance: Review of endocrine-disrupting mechanisms for cosmetic ultraviolet filters.
papers.caution: Review spans in-vitro and in-vivo studies across organisms, not direct causal human evidence.

papers.relevance: Three-day HERMOSA personal-care product replacement intervention in adolescent girls.
papers.caution: Some biomarkers did not decrease and two low-level parabens increased unexpectedly.

papers.relevance: NHANES mouthwash and sunscreen use associated with urinary phthalate and phenol biomarkers.
Repeated measurements of men's product use and urinary phthalate/paraben biomarkers.
papers.caution: Associations concentrated in MEP and parabens rather than all measured metabolites.

papers.relevance: HERMOSA baseline product-use predictors of urinary endocrine-disrupting chemical biomarkers.
papers.caution: Same cohort as PMC5047791; the two papers are not independent replications.

papers.relevance: Phthalate and volatile-organic-compound content in sanitary pads and diapers.
papers.caution: Product-content study with modeled absorption, not a measured endocrine outcome.

papers.relevance: Volatile organic compounds and modeled health risks in feminine hygiene products.
papers.caution: Primarily toxicological risk modeling rather than endocrine-disruption evidence.

papers.relevance: Prenatal hair-product use and gestational age at delivery.
papers.caution: No chemical measurement; one late-pregnancy association cannot establish an EDC mechanism.

papers.relevance: Hair-product use and maternal sex-steroid/thyroid hormones during pregnancy.
Receptor-assay evidence of hormonal activity in Black hair-care product mixtures.
papers.caution: In-vitro extracts do not establish in-vivo dose or disease.

papers.relevance: Occupational nail-salon phthalate and volatile-organic-compound exposure.
papers.caution: No hormonal endpoint and not direct household hygiene-product use.

papers.relevance: Menstrual blood as a non-invasive matrix for endocrine-disrupting chemical biomonitoring.
papers.caution: No product source; one reported DEP range is internally inconsistent and must not ground questions.
